denial_pattern_sources: appeal_closed_as_withdrawn: State Insurance Commissioner complaint logs (CA DOI 2022, NY DFS 2023)
wrong_benefit_category: CMS Essential Health Benefits Bulletin 2022; documented in Aetna member complaints
denial_letter_text:
Dear Member,

We received your provider's request on 03/09/2026 for Myomectomy for fertility preservation related to Symptomatic uterine fibroids (D25.9). After review, we cannot approve this request.

Our review applied Aetna Clinical Policy Bulletin (CPB) criteria and InterQual review standards. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. The requested frequency exceeds plan guidelines for Symptomatic uterine fibroids (D25.9). Additional visits are not medically necessary at this cadence.

Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the utilization-management requirements associated with frequency excessive. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. The requesting provider may submit additional records, but the current file does not support approval.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials.

You have the right to appeal this determination. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this letter. Include your member identification number and the service reference shown on your explanation of benefits.

Sincerely,

Aetna Medical Management

Please note that benefits are subject to all terms, exclusions, and limitations of your group health plan document. This letter constitutes the formal adverse determination for the service identified above.
clinical_context: The 42-year-old member has Symptomatic uterine fibroids (D25.9). He has been treated under commercial coverage with Myomectomy for fertility preservation recommended as the next appropriate step. Heavy bleeding Hgb 9.8; failed hormonal management; desires pregnancy. This directly contradicts the insurer's position that frequency excessive bars coverage: the chart documents dates, doses, and objective findings that were submitted with the original request submitted in Q1 2026 but are not reflected in the denial letter's rationale. Treating clinicians note ongoing functional impairment and avoidable harm if his care is delayed further.
